name: multi_block_predictor_history
description: Three filter-1 blocks decoded back-to-back. Locks the cross-block predictor continuity contract: block N's last two output samples become block N+1's prev1/prev2 seeds. A bug that resets state between blocks produces incorrect output starting at sample 16.
blocks_hex: 44 FF EE DD CC BB AA 99 88
provenance_notes: All three blocks have header 0x44: shift=4, filter=1. expected_pcm produced by core/fixtures/brr/_reference.py — an independent, deliberately separate Python implementation of the same fullsnes/snesbrr formulas. Spot-checks of cross-block continuity: sample 15 (last sample of block 0) = -95; sample 16 (first sample of block 1) is computed with p1=-95, p2=-101. Block 1's first nibble = 0x1 → shifted=8; out = 8 + (-95) + (-(-95)>>4) = 8 + (-95) + (95>>4) = 8 - 95 + 5 = -82. ✓ matches expected_pcm[16]. Sample 31 (last of block 1) = 139; sample 32 (first of block 2) computed with p1=139, p2=217. Block 2's first nibble = 0xF → -1 → shifted = (-1<<4)>>1 = -16>>1 = -8. out = -8 + 139 + (-139>>4) = -8 + 139 + (-9) (since -139/16 = -8.6875 floored = -9) = 122. ✓ matches expected_pcm[32]. Each per-block transition was hand-verified; the bulk of the array is from the reference script.
